task_completion.description: The PostgreSQL database service (acme-db) is running successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output of `docker ps` or `docker compose ps` showing the `acme-db` container with an 'Up' status, or Docker logs indicating PostgreSQL is ready to accept connections.

task_completion.description: The Redis cache service (acme-cache) is running successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the `acme-cache` container with an 'Up' status, or Docker logs indicating Redis has started.

task_completion.description: The Node.js API service (acme-api) is running successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the `acme-api` container with an 'Up' status, or Docker logs indicating the API server is listening on port 3000.

task_completion.description: The Nginx reverse proxy service (acme-nginx) is running successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the `acme-nginx` container with an 'Up' status, or Docker logs indicating Nginx has started.

task_completion.description: The Python background worker service (acme-worker) is running successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the `acme-worker` container with an 'Up' status, or Docker logs indicating the Python worker is running.

task_completion.description: The API responds correctly at http://localhost:8080/api/products.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a successful `curl` command to `http://localhost:8080/api/products` returning a JSON response with product data, or a web browser displaying the same endpoint with the correct JSON output.

integrity.description: The agent used genuine Docker commands to run the services.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent executing commands like `docker compose up`, `docker compose ps`, or `docker ps` in the terminal, with standard Docker output formatting.

integrity.description: The agent verified the API endpoint using a real HTTP client.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent using `curl`, `wget`, or a web browser to access `http://localhost:8080/api/products`, rather than just echoing a hardcoded string.

integrity.description: The agent modified the configuration files manually or via standard commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent using text editors (like nano, vim) or commands (like sed) to edit `docker-compose.yml` and `nginx.conf`, rather than downloading pre-made solution files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing 5 specific bugs to make the application run: 1) In docker-compose.yml, change POSTGRES_DATABASE to POSTGRES_DB. 2) In docker-compose.yml, change the api service network from 'backend-net' to 'app-network'. 3) In docker-compose.yml, change REDIS_URL from 'redis:6379' to 'redis://cache:6379'. 4) In nginx.conf, change the upstream server port from 3001 to 3000. 5) In docker-compose.yml, change the worker command from 'python -m app.tasks' to 'python -m worker.tasks'. The final API endpoint http://localhost:8080/api/products should return a JSON object containing a list of products.
